★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 1.68 2.46 2.63 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.16 0.43 0.25 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.34 0.55 0.54 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.17 1.48 1.84 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 0.50 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-11-22 37 34 8 438 3 745
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-04-28 43 32 16 332 2 498
Total weighted health score
683.3
lower is better

Deficiencies (99)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

93 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
4 G/H/I Actual harm
2 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected

0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2025-01-14 2025-02-22
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0570 Assure the security of all personal funds of residents deposited with the facility. E 2024-11-22 2025-02-22
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. E 2024-11-22 2025-02-22
0678 Provide basic life support, including CPR, prior to the arrival of emergency medical personnel , subject to physician orders and the resident’s advance directives. E 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. E 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2024-11-22 2025-02-22
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. E 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-11-22 2025-02-22
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. F 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. D 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. D 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. F 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. E 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0887 Educate residents and staff on COVID-19 vaccination, offer the COVID-19 vaccine to eligible residents and staff after education, and properly document each resident and staff member's vaccination status. E 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. E 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. E 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. F 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. F 2024-11-22 2025-01-05
